customers.context: Delaware law, provisions in our governing documents and our agreement with Microsoft could delay or prevent a change in control. Under our agreement with Microsoft for the Xbox, if someone makes an offer to purchase at least 30% of our outstanding common stock, Microsoft may have first and last rights of refusal to purchase the stock.

single_source_dependencies.context: We utilize CoWoS technology for semiconductor packaging. We utilize foundries, such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited, or TSMC... to produce our semiconductor wafers.

geographic_concentration.context: Our business depends on our ability to receive consistent and reliable supply from our overseas partners, especially in Taiwan and South Korea. While currently our supply chain is mainly concentrated in Asia, we are expanding into the U.S. and Latin America.
Our business depends on our ability to receive consistent and reliable supply from our overseas partners, especially in Taiwan and South Korea.

geographic_concentration.activity: research and development, sales, and administrative purposes
geographic_concentration.context: We also own and lease facilities for data centers, research and development, and/or sales and administrative purposes throughout the U.S. and in various international locations, primarily in China, India, Israel, and Taiwan.

geographic_concentration.activity: research and development, operations, and sales and marketing
geographic_concentration.context: We are monitoring the impact of the geopolitical conflict in and around Israel on our operations, including the health and safety of our approximately 6,000 employees in the region who primarily support the research and development, operations, and sales and marketing of our networking products.

capacity_constraints.context: We have previously experienced and may continue to experience extended lead times of more than 12 months. To secure future supply and capacity, we have paid premiums, provided deposits, and entered into long-term supply agreements and capacity commitments.
We expect supply constraints to be a headwind to Gaming in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 and beyond.

supply_chain_risks.context: Over the past three years, we have been subject to a series of shifting and expanding export control restrictions, impacting our ability to serve customers outside the United States. As of the end of fiscal year 2026, we were effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing/compute market.

While currently our supply chain is mainly concentrated in Asia, new and existing export controls or changes to existing export controls could limit alternative manufacturing locations and negatively impact our business.
In periods of shortages impacting the semiconductor industry and/or limited supply or capacity in our supply chain, the lead times for certain supply may be extended.

revenue_concentration.context: For fiscal year 2026, sales to one direct customer represented 22% of total revenue... all of which were primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment.
sales to another direct customer represented 14% of total revenue, all of which were primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment.

geographic_revenue.context: United States: $149,617 million. Revenue from sales to customers headquartered outside of the United States accounted for 31% and 41% of total revenue for fiscal years 2026 and 2025, respectively.
Taiwan: $42,345 million. In fiscal year 2026, we estimate 76% of Data Center revenue from Taiwan-headquartered customers was attributed to end customers based in the United States and Europe.

purchase_obligations.context: As of January 25, 2026, these commitments were $95.2 billion, of which substantially all will be paid through fiscal year 2027.

Multi-year cloud service agreement commitments as of January 25, 2026, were $27 billion, for which $7 billion, $6 billion, $5 billion, $5 billion, $2 billion, and $2 billion will be paid in fiscal years 2027, 2028, 2029, 2030, 2031, and 2032 and thereafter, respectively.

market_risk_disclosures.exposure: fixed-rate investment portfolio and outstanding debt
market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 0.5% decrease in yield curve = $0.2 billion decrease in fair value
market_risk_disclosures.context: According to our sensitivity analysis on our investment portfolio, a decrease in the yield curve of 0.5% as of the end of fiscal year 2026 and 2025 would decrease the fair value for these investments by approximately $0.2 billion.

market_risk_disclosures.sensitivity: 10% USD strengthening = $180 million adverse impact on AOCI
market_risk_disclosures.context: If the U.S. dollar strengthened by 10% as of January 25, 2026 and January 26, 2025, the amount recorded in Accumulated other comprehensive income (loss) related to our foreign exchange contracts before tax effect would have been an adverse impact of $180 million and $136 million, respectively.
